https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Lerian.content: Go Standards Index. New feature modules: core.md, bootstrap.md, domain.md, quality.md. Relevant task modules: architecture.md, domain-modeling.md, testing-unit.md, security.md, multi-tenant.md, compliance.md. Full module URLs are listed in the standards index and should be passed from this cached entry rather than re-fetched per gate.

review_scope_policy.rationale: T-001 Gate 8 used the origin/main...HEAD cumulative diff (207 files, +11,560 lines) and spiraled to 25 iterations remediating Ring-mandated platform compliance unrelated to the 961-line characterization deliverable. From T-002 on, Gate 8 reviews ONLY the diff from each task's start commit to HEAD ({task.review_baseline_commit}...HEAD), confining reviewers to that task's own changes. Reviewers MUST NOT review pre-existing branch delta vs origin/main.
review_scope_policy.instruction_for_gate8: When dispatching ring:codereview at Gate 8, set the review target to `git diff {task.review_baseline_commit}...HEAD` (the current task's commits only). Do NOT diff against origin/main. Pass the baseline commit explicitly to every reviewer.

develop_reconciliation.conflicts_resolved: Only go.mod + go.sum (dependency versions). Kept HEAD lib versions (all >= develop: lib-auth v2.8.0, lib-commons v5.2.0 pinned, lib-license-go v2.3.5) plus our-only lib-observability v1.0.0 + lib-streaming v1.3.1; go mod tidy regenerated go.sum. One test-only merge-fallout fixed by backend-engineer: get_connection_schema_test.go:698 missing 6th arg (MultiTenantEnabled bool) for develop's refactored NewGetConnectionSchema signature.
develop_reconciliation.verification: go build clean; go vet clean; all test binaries compile; go test ./... = 54 ok / 0 FAIL; make lint = 0 issues.
develop_reconciliation.tenant_model_finding: AUTHORITATIVE develop connection model post-merge contradicts the pre-merge plan default of (tenant, org, product, configName). Evidence: (1) pkg/model/connection.go Connection struct has ProductName (required attribute) but NO OrganizationID and NO TenantID field. (2) The product ENTITY/table was removed (commit a6b8339, 'make connections standalone'); ProductName survives only as a string label. (3) pkg/mongodb/connection/indexes.go: unique index on config_name ALONE (partial deleted_at==nil), per-database. (4) pkg/mongodb/connection/connection.mongodb.go FindByName filters by config_name + deleted_at ONLY — no product/org/tenant filter. Tenant isolation is PHYSICAL (DB-per-tenant via tmcore), not a field. => Effective connection identity = (tenant_DB, config_name). Product is non-scoping; org absent. The Engine (in-process, cannot do DB-per-tenant) must carry tenantId logically. B2 scope-key decision escalated to owner: this finding contradicts both the plan default and the owner's stated 'tenantId > orgs/products' hierarchy.
develop_reconciliation.b2_decision: RESOLVED by owner 2026-06-01: drop org AND product from the Engine entirely. Rationale: the Engine is embedded INTO each product (matcher, reporter, ...), so the product IS the host process — it does not need to identify itself as 'product' inside its own embedded Engine, and org is the embedder's business grouping, not an Engine isolation concern. One embedded product may serve N tenants, so tenantId REMAINS the isolation boundary (owner: 'tenant TEM que estar no contexto', non-negotiable). DECISION: TenantContext = {TenantID, RequestID} (remove OrganizationID and ProductName); connection scope key / uniqueness = (tenantId, configName). This SIMPLIFIES the T-002/T-003 surface (fewer scope dimensions). Owner's 'tenantId > orgs/products' hierarchy still holds at the business layer — orgs/products live ABOVE the Engine in the host/product layer; the Engine sits below the product. CARRY-FORWARD for T-008/T-010: when wiring the legacy Manager onto the Engine, decide the disposition of the Manager's required ProductName field + X-Product-Name header (keep as host display attribute the Engine ignores, or remove).

phase_b.commit: 901159efix(engine): scope connections by tenantId, drop org/product from Engine model
phase_b.summary: TenantContext = {TenantID, RequestID}; removed OrganizationID, ProductName, and ConnectionDescriptor.ProductName. Scope key + config-name uniqueness re-keyed to (tenantID, configName). tenantId validation re-implemented locally as a value (byte-equivalent to lib-commons tenant-manager/core.IsValidTenantID + MaxTenantIDLength=256, ^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*$) WITHOUT importing tenant-manager (verified via go list -deps: no mongo-driver/dbresolver/tenant-manager transitive). Folded in 3 prior T-003 MEDIUMs (Delete-guard symmetry, duplicate-create-with-protector, tenant-isolation-through-Engine). Coverage engine 96.0% / memory 92.7%, race-clean. Independently verified by orchestrator: commit atomic (pkg/engine only), zero forbidden transitive deps, repo build clean, engine tests green with -race.

phase_c.gate8_scope: git diff de2fb12..HEAD -- pkg/engine/ (19 files, ~4513 lines). Baseline de2fb12 + path filter neutralizes the develop merge (pkg/engine absent on develop, so the merge did not touch it). Full Engine surface: T-002 contracts + T-003 lifecycle + Phase B correction reviewed as one coherent unit so multi-tenant-reviewer sees TenantContext and the store scope key together.
phase_c.gate8_conditional_specialists: none triggered (library/contract code; no observability/streaming/systemplane runtime)
phase_c.gate8_iteration_1: ISSUES_FOUND. 8 PASS; test-reviewer FAIL with 2 HIGH + 2 MEDIUM. HIGH-1: test name 'DuplicateWithProtectorIsRejectedBeforeReEncrypt' asserted an ordering the code lacked (protector called 2x, encrypt-then-reject); aligned with security LOW-3 (wasted-encrypt/DoS vector). HIGH-2: B4 finding #1 (guard not consulted on missing connection) had no behavioral test. MEDIUM-1: validateTenantScope error branch tested only on Create. MEDIUM-2: Delete-blocked test did not assert checker tenant scope. multi-tenant-reviewer re-verified the tenantId-only model FROM SCRATCH (prior org==tenant PASS invalidated) and confirmed PASS.
phase_c.gate8_iteration_2: PASS. Remediation commit cdead4a: CreateConnection reordered to reject-before-encrypt (existence pre-check before protectSecret; store.Create retained as atomic uniqueness backstop) — closes HIGH-1 + security LOW-3; behavioral guard-on-missing tests added for Delete+Update (HIGH-2); validateTenantScope hardened to call isValidTenantID + table-driven invalid-tenant test across all 5 ops (MEDIUM-1 + security LOW-1); Delete-blocked test asserts checker tenant scope (MEDIUM-2); isNilPort unreachable reflect.Interface case dropped (code LOW). 210 tests pass, race-clean, engine 97.4% / memory 92.7%, vet/lint/build clean, dependency_test passes. Re-reviewed 7 surface-affected reviewers (test, code, business-logic, security, nil-safety, multi-tenant, performance) — all PASS, 0 issues; dead-code + lib-commons unaffected (PASS from iter 1).
carry_forward_low: Limits partial-defaulting all-or-nothing (engine.go) — fix when limits consumed (T-006/T-007).

IsMultiTenant() misnomer + structural collision with tmcore.MultiTenantChecker interface (same signature — a host embedding TenantContext would accidentally satisfy it). API-surface/breaking change; defer to a dedicated cleanup.
TenantResolver port wired but inert — wire when extraction/schema ops land (later subtask).

Real MongoDB ConnectionStore adapter (T-008) MUST carry a unique index on (tenantId, config_name); also the find-then-create pre-check becomes a redundant round-trip there — consider insert-and-handle-conflict for that adapter.
No explicit concurrent-create test (backstop proven by inspection + -race) — optional add.
dependency_test.go allow-lists tenant-manager/core by import path but that pkg pulls mongo-driver+dbresolver; harden to assert on go list -deps (carried from T-001).

The credential-decrypt-at-host-seam design (ST-T004-03 deviates from literal spec 'decrypt in Engine') was INDEPENDENTLY BLESSED by business-logic (PASS, 'correct, strictly better') and security (PASS, plaintext lifetime minimal, core never sees ciphertext). nil-safety HIGHs: typed-nil factory from registry + Build returning (nil,nil) both reachable to panic (host-provided contract).

subtask_commits.ST-T005-01: 1bd08b7 — Engine DiscoverSchema (cache-first: ConnectionStore -> tenant-scoped SchemaCache (scope,configName) -> ConnectorFactory.Build -> Connector.DiscoverSchema -> canonical normalize -> write-through). 13 tests, engine 95.8%.
subtask_commits.ST-T005-02: 46a6366 — Engine ValidateSchema + canonical ValidationReport/ValidationFailureType (datasource_not_found/table_not_found/field_not_found/invalid_filter/limit_exceeded). Shared resolveSchema extracted from DiscoverSchema. 255 tests, engine 97.3%.
subtask_commits.ST-T005-03: 1453880 — pkg/enginecompat/plugincrm schema compat adapter (CRM stays OUT of core; AST + dep-graph tests prove pkg/engine does not import it). adapter 100%.

t005_progress.gate8_scope: git diff 08b5c8e..HEAD (5 files: pkg/engine/{schema_ops.go,schema_ops_test.go,memory/cache.go} + pkg/enginecompat/plugincrm/{schema_adapter.go,schema_adapter_test.go}). pkg/engine boundary independently re-verified CLEAN via go list -deps (no redis/mongo/driver/etc).
t005_progress.gate8_conditional_specialists: none triggered — diff scan found no tracer/span/metric/event/outbox/runtime-config; library/contract+adapter code only.
review_flags_for_gate8: ARCH (ST-01): system-table filtering (pg_*, Oracle SYS, db_*) deliberately NOT in Engine core — pushed to host connector before SchemaSnapshot crosses boundary. business-logic-reviewer MUST confirm the connector-produced snapshot already excludes system tables, else we removed the filter rather than preserved it. Carry to T-008 risk register.

ARCH (ST-02): DB-type-specific name normalization (Oracle lowercasing, PG/SQLServer default-schema injection, plugin_crm collection auto-discovery) deliberately NOT in core — validates literal snapshot names. Must land in host connector/adapter at T-008 or Manager cases regress.

CONTRACT (ST-02): new SchemaValidationRequest with TYPED FilterFields (legacy never validated filters; avoids Engine parsing opaque Filters map[string]any DSL). Filter count reuses MaxFieldsPerTable budget (no separate limit). Additive — Manager not migrated.

BEHAVIOR (ST-03): adapter picks lexicographically-first prefix match (sorted) vs legacy non-deterministic map-iteration first-match. Strict improvement; business-logic to confirm production has no multi-match collections case. Adapter is destination seam, NOT yet wired into validate_schema.go (legacy transformPluginCRMTablesFromSchema still live; wiring is T-008/T-010).
t005_progress.gate8_iteration_1: ISSUES_FOUND. 7 PASS; code FAIL (1 MEDIUM dead exec.Command + 3 LOW); business-logic NEEDS_DISCUSSION (2 HIGH + 2 MEDIUM + 1 LOW). HIGH-1: nested/parent-object field matching dropped (exact membership vs legacy HasField prefix-of-dotted-path) -> false field_not_found for plugin_crm nested fields. HIGH-2: CRM adapter physical-name collision silently overwrites field lists (nondeterministic drop). MEDIUM-2: empty/whitespace names reported as table_not_found instead of malformed-request. MEDIUM-1 (limit-as-report) ACCEPTED as spec-aligned (ST-T005-02). security/multi-tenant/dead-code/lib-commons/test/nil-safety/performance PASS.
t005_progress.gate8_iteration_2: PASS 9/9. Remediation commit 04858cc (orchestrator ruled FIX on both HIGHs as correctness calls, not taste): FIX1 nested-field matching restored via fieldIndex{exact set + sorted slice} + sort.SearchStrings on lower-bound name+'.' (exact legacy HasField parity, '.' delimiter blocks false-prefix) for Fields AND FilterFields; FIX2 CRM collision now unions field lists deterministically (sorted iteration, de-duped) instead of overwrite; FIX3 empty/whitespace name guard -> CategoryValidation before membership; FIX4 dead exec.Command deleted; FIX5 distinct field-vs-filter Detail assertions; FIX6 (nil,nil) connector build test. Re-reviewed 7 surface-affected reviewers (business-logic, code, test, nil-safety, security, multi-tenant, performance) ALL PASS 0 issues; dead-code + lib-commons carried PASS (no new deps/removed symbols). engine 97.2% / plugincrm 100%, race-clean, 5255 tests, vet/lint/build clean, boundary go list -deps clean.
carry_forward: T-008 RISK: system-table exclusion (pg_*, Oracle SYS, db_*) deliberately NOT in Engine core — host connector MUST exclude system tables before SchemaSnapshot crosses boundary, else they appear as valid tables. Verify at T-008 connector wiring.

T-008 RISK: DB-type name normalization (Oracle lowercasing, PG public./SQLServer dbo. default-schema injection) deliberately NOT in core — Engine validates literal snapshot names. Host connector (or per-DB compat adapter like plugincrm) MUST normalize before snapshot, else Oracle/PG/SQLServer unqualified-name validation regresses.

T-008 MIGRATION NOTE: limit breach is a ValidationReport failure (Valid:false, limit_exceeded), NOT a hard error (matches ST-T005-02 spec). Legacy Manager returned HTTP 422. Host must map limit_exceeded report failures -> appropriate HTTP status when wiring.

T-008 WIRING: plugincrm schema adapter (MapTablesForCRMCompatibility) is the DESTINATION seam, NOT yet wired into validate_schema.go; legacy transformPluginCRMTablesFromSchema still live. Wire + delete legacy at T-008/T-010 (then re-run dead-code on the orphaned legacy path).

PERF LOW (carried): CRM resolvePhysicalCollection is O(L*P) and indexSnapshotFields eagerly indexes all snapshot tables (request-unbounded P). Bounded/linear, single-pass; optimize only if a wide-snapshot benchmark flags it.

MEDIUM (security maintainability smell): memory.SchemaCache GetErr/PutErr are exported error-injection fields on a production-named type (test affordance). Consider moving to a test-only double when the real Redis adapter lands.
filter count reuses MaxFieldsPerTable budget (no separate filter limit) — revisit if filters need their own budget.

t006_progress.tenant_model_note: STALE SPEC ALERT: T-006 subtask specs say 'tenant/product scope' and ST-T006-03 says carry OrganizationID into the plan + read pkg/resolver/resolver.go. Per LOCKED B2 decision, org/product do NOT exist in the Engine. In the Engine this is TENANT scope only: plan carries tenantID + RequestID; scope enforcement is validateTenantScope; NO org/product reintroduced. ST-T006-03 'product scope' == tenant scope.

subtask_commits.ST-T006-01: fc0b440 — Engine.PlanExtraction: pure deterministic planner, ExtractionRequest -> secret-free ExtractionPlan. Reuses T-005 ValidateSchema/resolveSchema (cache-first), sorts all keys, attaches filters per owning table path, preserves metadata.source, carries tenantID+RequestID only. 17 tests, engine 97.5%, race-clean, boundary clean (go list -deps), full suite 5272.
subtask_commits.ST-T006-02: 2b0eb47 — effective-limits resolution + per-request overrides. Limits.Effective(overrides) with copy-semantics (override default-mutation impossible); override ceiling = Engine default; planner-owned dims (concurrency/timeout/result-size) enforced in Effective; count limits stay in limitFailures via new validateSchemaWithLimits run against effective limits; ConnectorHardLimits map carried-not-interpreted for host adapter. RIPPLE: Limits now non-comparable with == (map field) -> 2 tests moved to reflect.DeepEqual; breaking only for any host doing ==Limits (none today). 300 tests, engine 96.1%, race-clean, boundary clean, suite 5291.
subtask_commits.ST-T006-03: b97a108 — tenant-scope consistency in planner. requireScopedConnections resolves every referenced config in tenant scope BEFORE limits/schema; cross-tenant + truly-unknown return BYTE-IDENTICAL CategoryNotFound (no existence oracle). Found+closed a request-shaping oracle: scope was previously a side-effect of validateSchemaWithLimits so the limit gate ran first (cross-tenant+limit-breach -> CategoryValidation vs CategoryNotFound). NO org/product added (TenantIDPropagatedNoOrgOrProduct proves). engine 96.7%, boundary clean, Manager command green.
subtask_commits.REMEDIATION (gate8 iter1): 97a28a0 — fix(engine): harden planner limits. 9 fixes: HIGH IsZero now counts ConnectorHardLimits (was silently clobbered by DefaultLimits); MEDIUM-sec bound datasource count <= MaxDatasources+1 BEFORE existence probe (CWE-770); MEDIUM-test rename UnknownConfigAfterLimits + 3 override-rejection tests w/ field-path assert; MEDIUM-code sort filter fields (determinism); LOW reject empty FieldSelection; LOW Limits non-comparable doc; LOW t.Parallel on 53 tests; LOW blank-name+store-error branch tests. engine 97.7%, race+shuffle clean, suite 5314.

st_t006_01_contract_flags_for_gate8: Extended existing T-002 ExtractionPlan/PlanStep (data-only placeholders) instead of new types: PlanStep gains Fields map[string][]string + Filters map[string]map[string]any; ExtractionPlan gains TenantID, RequestID, Metadata. omitempty preserves prior serialized shapes.
PlanExtraction builds a SchemaValidationRequest and calls Engine.ValidateSchema — single source of cache-first resolution; no duplicated resolveSchema.

Error mapping: non-valid report -> CategoryValidation, EXCEPT ValidationDatasourceNotFound -> CategoryNotFound (unknown connection); source-down keeps CategoryUnavailable; empty mapped fields -> CategoryValidation.
Plan carries tenantID + RequestID only (NO org/product per LOCKED context) + copied Metadata preserving metadata.source (plugin_crm).
Immutability-by-convention: returned slices/maps are independent copies (sortedCopy, deep filter copy, copyMetadata), proven by PlanCopiesAreImmutable.
Seams left clean for ST-T006-02 (limits read from e.options.limits into plan) and ST-T006-03 (scope via validateTenantScope/ValidateSchema) — those subtasks EXTEND, not rewrite.
t006_progress.state_integrity_note: current-cycle.json had INVALID JSON from commit b963073 (pre-T-002, prior session) through fc0b440 — an extra closing brace at the end of the T-002 agent_outputs block (}}} should be }}). Nothing machine-parsed the file so the dev-cycle proceeded on it as text. Fixed 2026-06-01 (commit 591bf6c); file now validates via jq/python. No data lost.
t006_progress.gate8_conditional_specialists: none triggered (diff scan: no tracer/span/metric/event/runtime-config; library/contract code).
t006_progress.gate8_iteration_1: ISSUES_FOUND. code FAIL (1 HIGH IsZero-drops-ConnectorHardLimits + 2 MEDIUM double-store-resolution + unsorted-filter + 1 LOW); test FAIL (2 MEDIUM misleading-name + override-rejection-untested + 2 LOW); security 1 MEDIUM (CWE-770 unbounded probe) + 2 LOW; business-logic 1 LOW (vacuous plan); multi-tenant/nil-safety/dead-code/performance/lib-commons PASS.
t006_progress.gate8_iteration_2: PASS 9/9. Remediation 97a28a0 (9 fixes). Re-reviewed 7 surface-affected (code, test, security, multi-tenant, business-logic, nil-safety, performance) ALL PASS 0 issues; dead-code + lib-commons carried PASS. multi-tenant did adversarial U1/U2 oracle re-proof after the FIX-2 reorder (cross-tenant vs unknown byte-identical preserved). engine 97.7%, race+shuffle clean.
carry_forward: T-008 PERF: double store-resolution per datasource (requireScopedConnections scope gate + validateDatasource) — KEPT by decision (closes existence oracle via scope-before-limits ordering), now bounded <= MaxDatasources+1 by the CWE-770 fix. Memoize first-pass descriptors when the DB-backed ConnectionStore lands (halves store round-trips on the valid plan path).

T-007 HOST-SEAM: ExtractionPlan metadata/filter values are shallow-copied (map containers independent, any-values shared by reference). Safe (opaque non-secret host data). If the plan is treated as an immutable snapshot downstream, deep-copy at the execute-time adapter.
T-007 HOST-SEAM: ConnectorHardLimits is carried into the plan but NOT interpreted by the core (driver-free map[string]int by design). The host adapter MUST enforce it at execute time.

T-007 TRANSPORT: ExtractionPlan.Steps / PlanStep.Fields / Metadata can be nil for empty cases; nil maps serialize to null not {} — handle at the host JSON-transport adapter if the plan crosses a wire.

OBSERVATION (not a fix): planValidationError's ValidationDatasourceNotFound branch is unreachable in PlanExtraction's flow (scope gate catches it first) but reachable via the standalone ValidateSchema entry point — defensive, kept.

tasks.title: Extract embedded connection lifecycle and credential protection
tasks.gate9_validation: PASS — success criteria + DoD aggregated as MET; Gate 8 PASS 9/9 in 2 iterations (see develop_reconciliation.phase_c). User APPROVED 2026-06-01. Scope deviation accepted: original 'preserve product ownership' superseded by locked B2 decision (tenant is the sole ownership/isolation boundary; org/product removed from Engine). T-002 lightly re-validated — TenantContext contract is stronger, still importable/dependency-light.

blocking_issues.description: config-name uniqueness diverges from Manager: Engine enforces per-(org,product), Manager FindByName is GLOBAL (no product filter). Comment falsely claims it mirrors Manager. Migration (T-008) would silently widen uniqueness. AWAITING USER DECISION: keep per-product (correct multi-tenant) + fix comment + document divergence, OR enforce global for Manager parity.

DeleteConnection guards active-execution checker BEFORE existence check (Update checks existence first). Asymmetric; delete-of-missing invokes checker and checker error masks not-found as CategoryUnavailable. Fix: make Delete symmetric (Find->not-found->guard->Delete).
Missing test: duplicate Create WITH protector enabled (protector runs before dup-check; assert existing ciphertext untouched + no second record).
Missing org-dimension isolation test (all tests use org-1; same product/different org should be isolated records).

low_issues: ConnectionPatch cannot express field-clearing (omit/clear/null mapping - pin at T-008)
keyVersion 0 ambiguous between unprotected and key-v0 (pin before T-008 Reveal)
ActiveExecutionChecker keyed by connectionID vs Manager bare ConfigName (host adapter reconciles)

tasks.title: Add extraction planner with limits and tenant/product scope checks
tasks.tenant_model_note: STALE SPEC ALERT (esp. ST-T006-03): subtask specs say 'product scope', 'product ownership', 'carry organization ID into plan' — these CONTRADICT the locked B2 decision (org AND product removed from Engine; tenantId is the SOLE isolation boundary). Mechanical translation per B2: product/org scope -> TENANT scope (tenantID, configName); 'carry tenant ID and organization ID' -> carry tenantID ONLY; 'mismatched product' -> cross-tenant; safe not-found must not leak cross-TENANT existence. The planner applies the same tenant-scope discipline as T-003/T-005 (validateTenantScope + store keyed by (tenantID, configName)). metadata.source (e.g. plugin_crm) IS preserved (legit worker compat). Deterministic sort + no-connector-exec-in-planner guards retained. Not a new decision — consistent application of locked B2.
